reviews.review_text:
cartoonishly bad story, so bad in fact that even the voices of the antagonists sound like the most generic cartoon bad guy
janky, unresponsive combat that feels as if i'm just swinging a pillow at the enemy regardless of the weapon
sluggish parkour with the gravity resembling more that of our moon, which eventually gets completely diminished anyway by the paraglider, which is also jank
the grappling hook feels like it's actively slowing me down instead of helping me move around the city
constant qte in parkour to reach maximum movement speed which is cool at first but gets really annoying when you just want to go fast to your next quest
weird ass rpg elements being mixed into a first-person zombie survival game feel ultimately pointless and have no impact on gameplay whatsoever apart from misleading the player into caring about their "build" because of some random whatever 2% buffs

it's dying light 1 but just objectively worse, even in the story department - which is Dying Light 2's only significant achievement

Dying Light 2: Reloaded Edition is a game that shines brightest when you stop focusing on the story and just enjoy running across rooftops while smashing zombies in the face.
The parkour system is easily the best part of the game. Moving through the city feels smooth, fast, and incredibly satisfying once you unlock more movement abilities. Chaining jumps, wall-runs, glides, and grapples together makes traversal genuinely fun even after dozens of hours.

Combat is also enjoyable, especially once you start getting stronger weapons and upgrades. Sending infected flying off rooftops never really gets old, and nighttime exploration creates some genuinely tense moments when stronger infected start chasing you through the streets.

Compared to launch, the Reloaded Edition feels much more polished and feature-complete. A lot of improvements and updates helped make the overall experience better, especially for co-op and combat. 

The good:
- Excellent parkour and movement system
- Traversal feels incredibly satisfying
- Fun and brutal melee combat
- Great co-op experience
- Large open world with lots to explore
- Nighttime gameplay creates real tension
- Tons of weapons, upgrades, and gear
- Visuals and city design are impressive
- Much improved compared to launch
- Dropkicking zombies remains amazing

The bad:
- Main story is pretty forgettable
- Dialogue and characters are inconsistent
- RPG loot system can feel unnecessary
- Open-world activities become repetitive
- Choices don’t impact the world as much as advertised
- Combat can feel floaty at times
- Some animations and physics feel awkward
- Bugs still occasionally happen
- Endgame grind can become tedious

Overall, Dying Light 2 is at its best when it fully embraces being a parkour zombie sandbox. The story may not leave a huge impact, but the movement, co-op gameplay, and chaotic nighttime exploration make it a very fun open-world experience.

We played the main story coop and I really have to open up a few things about this one.

Well, the game isn't bad. There is a LOT to do but the rewards are so lackluster that you really don't need to do those. We were almost max parkour and combat perks even with only the main quest line. So they really should have made the rewards...more rewarding. Heh. Like new weapon types ect. You unlock guns from a mission based system that you would have to grind so that was a strange design choice as well.
We just unlocked the first shotgun and pretty much steamrolled everything.
After a while you were stocked with so much stuff that all the more rewarding loot locations became irrelevant as well. I had plenty of batteries but my friend was battery starved the whole game so..RNG also plays a role how you want to upgrade stuff. Oh. and upgrading took Medium Infected Trophies that you always needed like 200x more to even upgrade your mods to your weapons.
The gameplay itself was really fun. Kicking stuff off buildings and the movement options felt great. Parkouring was smooth and with some levels in it it became even more smoother.
The NPCs were pretty annoying overall and the dialogue sometimes really gringe worthy. 
But the game is so damn buggy, CO-OP bugs were plentiful and every elevator became a death trap. Also
the game crashed a bunch, ending cutscenes in a black screen that you needed to ALT+F4 from.

In the end the main story was lengthy and there was a lot to do if you wanted. The action was fun but in the end geme lacked some more depth and force you more out of the main quest line to open up stuff. Perhaps lock you out from some of the parkour/combat moves until you did them or something.
I can still recommend this as a coop experience but I'd advise to wait for a good sale on this one.
tons of bugs, broken quests, questionable gameplay mechanics, horrible writing, microtransaction slop. but hey, at least the graphics are good!
7/10. Fantastic gameplay, mediocre story. Hakon is my goat.

Good traversal except in some buildings with weird architecture, and combat is ok too tho repetitive specially when you the same takedown animation multiple times in the same fight, story is no good, there are events that go unexplained and no one even mentions, there lots of time attack challenges as well which are very boring and the most simple quest you can come up with. Factions have little to no impact in gameplay, story and world progression. And another bad offender in open world games, random events, they have no interaction with the open world, its very often that you see 2 random events from opposing factions mere meters away from each other and they don't interact at all. Completely immersion breaking, and the DLC was so bad I had to disable it couldn't stand having a constant marker on my map for it. Overall, I would suggest going with the original one instead

The game is broken. My friend and I ran into two different game breaking main mission soft-lock glitches on both of our campaigns not allowing us to continue past the very beginning of the game. It shouldn't be legal to sell this product.
dlc crashes the game and bugs to a point where you can't move forward. the original game barely finishes with a million bugs but still playable. this is an L in 2026 lol

Not a patch on the first game.

The button layout is all over the place, especially on controller.

The level design makes unbroken free-running much more difficult, especially once you get to the second area. I was psyched to get a glider, but it was a huge disappointment, slow and clunky and it completely ruined the flow of gameplay.

There's a fixation on loot, but never anything good to find. I got loaded down with gear for a ranged class, but it took 16 hours to find a ranged weapon. Again, I got excited, except the first basic zombie I encountered tanked 5 arrows to the head without dying. The bow in the original was the absolute best weapon, with me throughout the entire game, I don't understand how they ♥♥♥♥♥♥ that up. I've read that it's only intended for human enemies (which makes no sense) - but this is a design error, as they didn't give us a weapon wheel, so are we expected to just constantly cycle weapons between targets?

It's odd, you can tell Techland put more work into this game, but in the wrong places.

this game is like if you took all the best parts of the first one made them worse and somehow made it more full of bugs. yeah man pay full price for stuff that doesnt work right. so cool. 

yeah there's more but i don't feel like writing it. posting this review after reluctantly starting up the game to try and keep going through the story and getting softlocked in some stairs in the first 5 minutes. just stick with the first one, at least the combat feels good in that one.
Whoever made this Parkour and movement in game, including flying should be left by his family, wife, kids and friends

This game is worse than DL1 in almost every aspect, having a coop campaign is normaly a redeaming factor but after several years of release I still get constant disconnections from friends in the same city (even when we use the same internet connection). It's an absolute disgrace to make your customers look for solutions themselves (that don't even work for every player).

If you want a good coop experience in a zombie sandbox, buy the first game.

I mean, grab it if it's on sale and you really want a co op game to play with friends, but otherwise it's awfully dire - shove every Ubislop and modern open world game into a blender, put it on full blast, and you've got Dying Light 2:

- The first handful of hours are absolutely fine, enjoyable even, but then you unlock the second area, which completely ruins everything. It's extremely vast with massive amounts of space between everything, so you're given a handglider that allows wide traversal. At this point, a lot of the game just stops being a thing. You don't need to parkour anymore, you know one of this series' biggest sellings points, and zombies are mostly on the ground. The experience just becomes almost entirely flying from one rooftop to another as you go from mission point to mission point, not even really feeling like much a game anymore. I saw some Reddit comments that were like "erm just don't use the glider then?", "you control the buttons you press", etc and it's like okay. But the map is clearly designed around it? The designers clearly wanted you to use it? To top it all off, the glider's controls are awful and it feels bad to use lol

- Blackout simulator. In co-op, if a player interacts with something important, it'll fade your screen black while it teleports you there. It'll then fade your screen to black a second time to start the cutscene, which ends up being exhausting and borderline nauesating, especially when players can force you to teleport to them

- The story and cutscenes are a complete mess. There's so many characters to keep track of, some of which have similar names and to be honest, I don't really care about any of them either. Voice actors aren't bad though 

- The skill trees are initially quite interesting, adding some fun parkour moves. As I said though, the glider makes both combat and parkour much less common. Plus, the trees run out of steam half way through - there's only so many you can press F to instantly kill a zombie, and the parkour upgrades become overly niche 

- The final mission of the game was bizarrely buggy in co-op, compared to everything else. We were going up an elevator, then got teleported above it, which killed two of us and then trapped us in a box. People got left behind in inaccessible areas after cutscenes ended, and black screens lingered longer than they should've

- Just a lot of small QoL things are missing, such as being able to see your co-op partners easily in the world through icons

if you dont care about story and want a couple hours of satisfying gameplay that'll get boring and repetitive then buy this game. 

major complaints:
horrendous story that you would need to do mental gymnastics for it to make sense 
unfair gameplay unless you over-level 
near unplayable night time (played on nightmare) if you get caught (which you will 90% of the time) you will have to go to a safe house or else they will chase you to the ends of the earth or until you die. good luck killing the volatiles too cause if you dont have a gun you wont be able to kill one in time before 3 others jump you.
horrendous story like holy ♥♥♥♥ i need to reiterate how lame it was, why should igaf about lawan again?
repetitive endgame
unrewarding gameplay. level up? ok all the ♥♥♥♥ you  grinded for is now pointless so go grind some more bud
and again what the ♥♥♥♥ was the story dude, you go from a city scale disaster to a worldwide one and completely jumped the gun. 

Compliments:
good physics engine

This game is trash. Just pure trash. Its lost the serious tone of the first game and the parkour/movement and combat are horrible. The graphics feel like a Borderlands rip off. I got it in a bundle sell thinking it was a good deal but I don't know what techland was thinking when they made this thinking it was a sequel to the first because they just trashed the title with this crap.

The gameplay is a nice improvement from the first game, but the story is so bad it makes me think it's bad on purpose. By the end of the game I was already skipping cutscenes because the writing is so awful and cringy I couldn't take it. I did not care about any of the characters or anything that was going on in the world.

Still, it's a nice playground to kill some zombies in and remains the best parkour game IMO. The map is huge and the sense of freedom is pretty cool. It makes me think of what could it have been if they had more time to add different random events, because even though the map is big, the encounters are all basically the same.

Long story short, it's not worth it at full price, but it's a good game.
is not bad at all just meh, the soul of the first game has been lost, kind of how ubisoft manage their games. just to much of the same. characters are boring af

Save your money even if its on sale, not only a janky mess when it came to the parkour & combat but the amount of cutscenes are INSANE and half the time it felt like I was watching a movie rather than actually playing the game.

End point, had more fun drinking out of a bowl of lukewarm dog water than playing this.

(The throwing knifes were pretty good tho)
Not as good and the first but still good.
its a very good game but it runs poorly despite my pc being well in the system requirments

really enjoyed the first half of the game and then you get the paraglider and everything goes downhill.
I don't even have it in me to finish this mess.
5/10

Honestly still a very amazing experience and over all a fun game to play with friends or by yourself its difficulty scaling could have been a little better because it does get very easy once you have completed everything and have obtained the best possible weapons and gear in the game but over all still very fun to just absolutely throw a zombie out or off a building has some minor bugs in the game but very much never really noticeable definitely going to get every achievement once I i get more time on my hands

I cant recommend this game enough, although its launch was rough similar to Cyberpunk 2077 - This game is extremely fun and has many activities to do. Some of the quests can be repetitive but the main story is really good. I chose wrong and got the worst ending and I was still satisfied with the ending cause it tied up loose ends and gave characters more time in the spotlight. PLEASE GET THIS GAME.. maybe when there is a discount. EVERYONE PUT IT ON YOUR WISHLIST AND WAIT FOR A SALE ITS A MUST PLAY
if you like survival games, and zombies.. this is it. it's fun and addicting . if you don't want to have a fun game and get lost in it. don't get this game because it's a effing blast.
loved the game and happy my character gotten some love interest but where's the part where you kick the zombie baby?

There's this egregious feeling that I get when wanting to finish this game just to be done with it. I'm a completion-holic, and this game makes me go back againts my own goals. When I finish a game that was good, I look at my library to see what can i finish just to get it out of here, and I see this game and it just irks my nerves. 

Story just sucks. Combat feels nothing like the first. Missions are so repetitive. Woke agendas thrown into characters /story & DLC. Like the DLC... wtf was that?!  

Rather than completing this garbage agenda fest, I'll just delete it and be happy with not completing this one. There is plenty of better games to play!

I think DL2, in its current state, is a pretty solid game. It's really fun, the maps are big and detailed, and there's tonnes of side quests to do. Legend Levels add greatly to replay value, and Tower Raid and Bloody Ties DLC add good extra content as well.

Honestly, I was initially discouraged from ever playing it because it has such a bad reputation among Dying Light fans. But having sunk 90+ hours into it now, I can safely say that the negative opinions are a hangover from what the game was like at launch (undeniably, a janky disappointment).

The devs have put in so much work to polish the game since that it's almost unrecognizable now. It's far from perfect, but it sucks that the negative perception has persisted this long.

I would recommend DL2, it definitely deserves a second chance.

Dying Light is  an 8/10. It’s super fun, especially if you like zombies, parkour, and open-world games. Definitely worth playing. and when you get the paraglider don't try to slide onto a building and think you'll survive the fall damage just sucks. also the protagonist likes to squeeze eyes a lot.

By far... My favorite. People dislike it and they have their reasons for it but this game is really really good. I actually like the story. The parkour is my ABSOLUTE FAVORITE! I know I'm like... Years late but, being able to play this and beat it was a blast. Now, I can continue throwing zombies off a roof and drop kicking the ♥♥♥♥ out of some PK bastards lol 10/10

The prologue (while you are still with Spike) pulls you so well into a dangerous yet beautiful world that could have tugged at your heartstrings with ease. But then the rest of the game feels different and takes place in a city full of ugly chemicals and edgy ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ characters who use "♥♥♥♥" and "♥♥♥♥♥♥♥" as if these words were their main vocabulary. They and the story feel even more brain-dead than the zombies roaming around. I admit the story of Dying Light 1 isn't the greatest either, but as idiotic as it is at times, it at least manages to make you care about or enjoy memorable characters while feeling the thrill of a world falling apart and escalating. Dying Light 2, however, completely falls flat like a volatile in the sun due to its lackluster implementation, made even worse by cheap action scenes and twists that try and fail to revive its dead core. It all has been so insufferable that I was close to getting random cramps like the MC, hopefully waking up somewhere completely different like him.

Worldbuilding that feels generic and half-baked couldn't save it either. And neither could the gameplay, which actually isn't too shabby but pulls you out of immersion far too often. For example, you have a huge sledgehammer-like thingy in your hand and are ready to give a staggered zombie a juicy finisher, but then the MC proceeds to grab the zombie's head and gouge its eyes out. You (♥♥♥♥♥♥♥) serious?

And don't even get me started on the goofy and easiest boss fight ever at the end. But I have to say that the game in general feels too easy, even at higher difficulties that respawn bullet-sponge zombies right in your face. Whenever the story (only talking about the main story because I couldn't really care about the mostly boring side quests anymore) would actually get challenging, you're forcefully turned into beast mode to flyswat your way through it.

The graphics aren't bad, but they feel way too saturated and dark at times. The badly implemented automatic brightness adjustment (e.g., facing the sun and then looking away) is just another thorn in my eyes. It's almost ridiculous that Dying Light 2 is the first game in which I had to use GeForce graphic filters to be able to look at it.

Anyway, if you ever feel like getting infected by disappointment and turning into a monster full of frustration, please buy and play this game. Just don't if you prefer to stay human.

I can't for any reason recommend this game: it's a straight downgrade of the first and full of bugs that completely remove my enjoyment, from entering walls while parkouring to losing instantly all immunity while doing some quests for some reasons (while my friend is perfectly ok). I will finish this pile of excrements playing it with a friend but for me I would have dropped it
ı definitely recommend playıng the dlc also, spent 20 hours playıng the dlc and didnt regret a second of it. play the first DL and than the DL stay human and then the dlc.
Отличная игра не рекомендую берите не пожалеете слишком дорого и тем более первая часть показалась интереснее  ночи нестрашные в общем прыгаю по крышам ваще нормас

I have always been a fan of this franchise, and while this game doesn't always live up to the first game's legacy, it does a great job at being a Dying Light game. Will always recommend this game and the first one! 9/10!

Decided to play after a while, and imagine my surprize, now it is uplayeble. 
After recent RTX update now the game fas lags and horrable FPS drops to 1 per second, aspecially near PK structures. 
And you now what, this RTX garbage daes not even emprove things, only brings problems, such as:
 - Flickering shadows;
 - Immediate dark to light lightning swith when you exit buildings, a.i. visual bugs whith are very noticeable;
 - When entering PK rooftop outposts textures are silver colored, even the NPC's
 - FPS drops that makes the game unplayeble. I even tried lowest graphic setting, RTX off, Switching from direct x 11 to 12 and vice versa - still FPS drops.

I play on RTX 4070, Last time when I was playing (about 2 years ago) everithing was smooth and nice, now it is FPS drops and texture bugs. What the actual F ???

It took time for me to grow on this title from the first one but now i can proudly say that the game blew me away and i think that aiden is my favorite zombie game mc now. this game is not a waste of money.

Продолжение легендарного паркура и мясокомбината. По сравнению с первой частью боёвка и паркур стали приятнее и в целом более пластичными. Но зомби уже не удивляют и воспринимаются как что-то обыденное. Графика хорошая, музыка не бросается в уши, геймплей хороший. Моя оценка 7 ингибиторов из 10.
Fun gameplay, beautiful graphics, weak writing and story decisions.

Dying Light 2 Stay Human is just pure mediocore, The community has just too much expectations from the first game and putting in the second game, the story is what just makes the game good, everything is okay, explorations, a lot of contents and update throughout the year, It still hits the usual open world zombie game just like the last time.

Overall It's a good continuation of making the franchise alive even though it became a negativitiy from the majority of the community I still enjoyed it despite the supremacy pure horror that made us feel from the first game.

I am looking forward to play the Dying Light The Beast, I hope they can make more games like a prequel before the whole humanity wiped and much more. looking forward to this great game, Thank you Techland!

DL2 is complete disappoint from its predecessor. combat and parkour is slow. Even though there has been updates. Human enemies are tougher now. story is confusing and trash, choices are average and baffling towards the end of the story. copy paste environment. vertical parkour is the only good concept of the game. You gotta pay to get better bundles
gameplay - 6
enemies - 7
story-2
combat - 5
parkour - 6

Very interesting game, the graphics, the enviroment and storyline are great. Give it a try and see for yourself.

10/10, recommended for 18+ only.

The story was pretty terrible, dialog trees are all fake, they make you click every option instead of just a nice clean cut scene.

Gameplay itself was pretty fun at least, outscaled the main quest difficulty pretty quickly though. It was also really short. Probably could've done the main quest in less than 15 hours.

I hate how volatiles are everywhere, and everything aggros dozens of virals. I only recommend on a heavy discount.
cool game. might have its problems but i love the dying light games. keep at it. it's fun. the parkour is always what i come back to. the yapping is a bit much in this game

Was not as advertised originally but still just as good. Spent a lot of hours running around the city running, jumping, climbing, falling...... even did new game+. Plenty of side activities and quest to do and the community events were fun even the Tower Raid game mode had its moments. The story was alright some cringe moments not that much of a edge lord to complain or praise mostly impartial. Had a good premise of doing what you must in sacrificing things to find your sister while also trying to stay human where you tiptoe closer to turning/becoming a monster (story wise) but they updated cutscenes and even aspects that were advertised showed up in the game letting you make choices that influence the endings to quest or story missions that affected the world. The whole choosing which side gets what in specific zones was cool, I personally favored the survivors because of the parkour playground. Game made me feel like a mad acrobatic axe swinging zombie killing machine. Grappling hooks and gliders made traversing even more fun especially when your in the city center.
Great game, fun, going through it second time in row, but the fight after botle of vodka againt 3s, almost deleted this game, just annoying!!
super cool game getting back into it after a long time of not playing
Great game , luv the parkour and combat mechanics :)

Spamming E when you have to loot, Or avoid a zombie is not fun gameplay, It's just annoying. Zombies climb faster than you, And they always seems to know where you are. I think I've reinstalled this game three times during my 7hrs of gameplay because it just so infuriating. I've tried to like it, But it's hard to when the game mechanics are more annoying than fun.

ha 69. nice.. anyway, still glitchy asf, softlock is a common occurence. worth it though, its not ♥♥♥♥ like it used to allegedly be. still i prefer the zombies from dl1 and i assume the beast. this one  is more arcadey styled imo but its retaining my attention which means something

bought and played on launch on PS5, disappointing... a year later bought and played on my pc, so many improvements made by the developers, why release unfinished games if they're just that, unfinished buggy messes that get tons of backlash. love the dying light series, disappointed with the launch but the game has improved drastically

This game is really buggy as hell with both it's physics and animations getting stuck on obstacles or when crouching. Unable to move at all and having to restart the game in order for you to respawn to a different spot where you are no longer stuck. The gameplay is great, but even then, the AI of the NPC's, enemies, and allies are janky as hell. I played this game already on PS5, and wanted to re-experience the game on PC. Never encountered an issue with the animation bugs until i tried playing this game on PC.

- Story = solid
- Graphics = solid solid
- Parkour gameplay = SOLID SOLID SOLID 

- Battle gameplay = sucks (atleast against humans)
- Safezones = 80% suck (not realistic to have just one bed on a roof and UV lights and around you are Zombies everywhere) --> Dying light 1 was in this topic really good.

Worth to buy this if you are a huge fan of slaying Zombies

Just... It is beautiful. Thank you. Thank you for everything. You guys listened, and made it better. Love y'all!

(Not sure why this got hated on, it was really fun to play through, though the ending fight was goofy, and not really a fight, but I cared more about the story tbh. I loved the ending, everyone was saved, and I was able to leave with the baddie Lawan. Thanks Hakkon! You were a bro.)

Its a great game, better than dying light the beast in terms of size, parkour-able area, progression, and story

the story in dying light the beast is just this game's story but executed worse

the parkour in dying light the beast while its a good system there is only 1 area in the game it can truly shine in as opposed to this game where its the majority of the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ map
i open game  i do mission it go night i get chase by volatle i run i sleep it day i kill people i get chased by peacemaker i die 10/10 would try
overall, pretty great game, doesnt impress at much except parkour, but I think thats kind of the point.

Great fun slaying zombies, and aerial movements add up to an excellent enjoyment.
There are some bugs and glitches to be found still, such as sleeping at certain Nightrunner camps will trap you in a wall upon waking, forcing a main menu quit and reload.  Clipping into walls, being forced into crouch animations, or bodies being solid objects you cannot move over, can disrupt combat or movement in general.  Despite this I have enjoyed my time, and recommend others to try it out.  I would advise to get it on sale though.

The starting was really tough, but the moment i got the crossbow and stun bolt with tons of Molotov im just unstoppable even on the hardest difficulty. Everything else is just junk from then on. The final boss fight is just ♥♥♥♥. AI is just bad and keep doing the same leaping attack, i can just dodge and wack till the boss drops. And i have tons of ammo, but i dun see any guns. I still prefer Dying Light, which i still remember the fear when i was playing.

Parkour! Zombies! Bash! Loot! Quest! Fly! There should be lots of fun to be had here. But sadly the developers broke the game by putting volatiles (mini-bosses) EVERYWHERE at night. There's a lot of janky behaviour around how they spawn and reset too.

Seasoned players complained that nights were too "easy". The response was "volatiles everywhere lol". Now beginners will struggle with night activities (which are kind of important for gathering crafting materials), because volatiles when you have beginner gear are tanks and you are squishy. Seasoned players with all the gear and levels are STILL complaining because now they have to deal with hordes of volatiles which is "boring".

There are mods which attempt to restore the old night time balance, but ymmv (and they tend not to work with seasonal events). Purchase with caution.
The Story is very cool, I liked it a lot! With all the updates it has recieved it has a great replay value.  Performace on Linux Nobara is very good!
IF you like zombies, climbing, gore, and a lot of weapons, this is thee game for you!

Runs well, plays well. Good dialogues, addition of parachute is nice. The immunity timer so far hasn't felt like it adds any pressure to the game. So far I've been able to ignore it especially since it can be extended as boosters are collected.

i played this game during when it was bugy and glitchy, during that time it was pain to play this game but the devs did fix all of that. BUT that last achievement "Ultramarathon" was terrible. what i did was that i have ps4 controller put a few strings on it and let the character run around in circles and then went afk. 

During that time afk i fell asleep and then went to work, got home walked the dog, washed the dog, dryed the dog, vacuumed the house, did the loudry, and went to bed. 

The next day i went to work, got back home walked the dog, did a home workout, took a shower, cleaned the bathroom, updated my cv, and sent a few resumes and went to bed. 

The next day i went to work, got back home, waled the dog, cleaned the outside porch,stairs and balcony, got rid of an old ac unit and installed a new ac unit, and then went to bed. 

THE FINAL DAY i got free of work, got up late, i took a shower, got a hair cut, walked the dog, helped a neigbour with some work, painted the balcony, waited for the balcony to dry and drank a few beers, painted it again, took another shower, and FINALLY when i sat down infront of the computer and was doing some paper work i hear the holy sound of steam telling me ive done it!!!

so all in all its a good game it may not be for everyone, but i think its a good game. 

BUT whoever that dev made that achievement  "Ultramarathon" i hope you stub your toe 

peace
♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ kak game ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ locked me into a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ glitch that prevents me from progressing after 16 hours of gametime over a month of playing

Terrible connectivity issues playing co-op every single time I play this game. Sometimes to the point where I get kicked every couple of minutes. 'Game master has disconnected'. Absolutely frustrating and ludicrous. As for the game itself (single player): campaign is fine, but the parkour mechanics are clunky and the movement is inconsistent. The legend lvl/blueprint upgrade/loot grind is unreal. Inferior to the first game in every single way.
This is a very good game, so much fun. Not mouth to say, if its on sale BUY IT!!!
Good game, The new map is good parkour is great storyline was a not as ood as the first game honestly but i would play it when its on sale.

The parkour is solid, the visuals are fine, but that's where the positives end. 

For an "open world" using a parkour-based (freedom-of-movement) traversal loop, almost every mission boils down to "go to objective marker. Look for yellow which denotes the only path you're allowed to take. Kill whatever is there. End mission." It feels almost criminal to offer a game with such a free range of movement skills but disallow creative use across story missions.

Meanwhile, seamless outside-to-inside environments could be cool but after the first couple they start feel samey. Buildings are empty shells that offer uninteresting loot. The combat never changes after the first hour or so, so outside of replacing a constant slew of broken samey weapons you're not incentivized to explore. It's all very repetitive. If you've played 2 hours of the game you've seen everything it has to offer, but you've still got another 20 hours to go.

Ok, so the gameplay is mid. What about the story? Alas. No better.  You barely spend time with any of the characters, which makes it hard to care about them. With few exceptions, people are 2-dimensional caricatures. They don't act like PEOPLE half the time, making decisions that make no sense for themselves, let alone anyone else. Big moments are artificial twists that were telegraphed from a mile away. For a game that touts the idea of your choices defining the story, very little of what you do feels like it actually matters.
Welp, can't convert old save to new and game crashes, merci!

This game is another one of those Cyberpunk 2077 situations, where the launch was rough and the game had such good improvements later that it redeemed itself over time. Being a huge fan of Dead Island as a kid, I played the ♥♥♥♥ out of this game's predecessor from the time it came out and for years to come. Needless to be said, I had a major emotional investment in this game's well-being from release. I never shamed the developers for their rough release. Times are changing. Change does not come without growing pain. They, among all others like them everywhere, were and still are going through this era of change in the game development industry. Therefore I waited, and waited, until I knew based on the playerbase's feedback that it had been properly fledged as a Dying Light game, and then I played it. If you're thinking of getting this game and playing it at this time, the answer is undeniably a yes. Get it, play it, appreciate it for what it has become and not for what it was. There is no time but the present to enjoy the things in life.
P.S., have many more hours than shown.
This is factually the buggiest game I have ever played in my life, every handful of minutes I'll encounter a brand new bug. Despite that I've actually enjoyed my time with this game.

I Love the game it is good lot of stuff to do and find most side quest are semi unique some likeable characters depending on personal thought. and more i could rant about i have played steam and xbox a lot but my true issue and reason for this post is lag it happens sooo much and i have to close the game like very hour for it to stop thing is only on pc/steam never happened on xbox and it really really annoying ruins the immerson when you guy moves at the speed of a snail everything gets slow blurry then you just teleport 5 feet forwards so overall great game should try for people who like this kind of thing just really wish techland would fix this instead of new buyable bundles with real gear and weapons i dont want to pay more money for stuff you should be capable of working for in game or grinding on pilgrim outpost

I've played this on my Xbox for quite a few more hours and I have to say, This game is worth every cent. Loved the gameplay and the fighting, the story wasn't bad, and I loved how you could develop the world based on your choices.

The most fun parkour zombie game I have EVER played and by far the BEST Dying Light in the franchise. The movement is super smooth and the combat flows amazingly. Everything you do is so satisfying and the skill ceiling is super high so every time I boot up the game and just run through the parkour challenges or the combat challenges I learn something new almost every time. 10/10 game and I will continue to keep replaying it til the end of time.

No idea why people enjoy this game. The parry mechanic doesn't work 90% of the time, the save system is completely broken - got killed on a quest, selected Spawn at last story point and lost about 3 hours of gameplay and it's not recoverable. Why not have a normal auto-save system plus normal saves? - your weapons just constantly fall apart for no reason. Oh you just bought this nice metal sledge hammer  for several hundred or thousand gold? Whelp three hits and it's broken. 

Oh and the fighting system? Swing this crowbar four times and you can't do anything anymore because this character, who has probably been in a lot of fights giving he is a Pilgrim, is apparently suffering from severe muscle atrophy.

Fun gameplay but deeply flawed. The Bloody Ties DLC is is absolutely terrible. Tower raid is fun, but why can't I just run it with my own character? Feels bolted on. The story is bad and choices don't really matter in terms of how the game plays out. Choosing survivors is always a no brainer because it makes the parkour better. Despite all that the parkour and combat is fun and keeps me coming back for more.

I loved Dying Light 1 and played through the entire game many times, I can't say the same about Dying Light 2. The combat started out fairly challenging, but turned into a joke once certain abilities were unlocked such as dropkick and grapple throw. The story was an absolute mess, huge plot holes with every story mission, and the swing to a more RPG focused gameplay was not the correct decision. The premise of having your decisions have a wide impact on the world around you sounds great at first, but there are only ever two options at best and quite often I liked neither of them. I was highly disappointed in the ending I got and I was looking forward to replaying the final mission (which was an option available in the first game) just to find out that once you finish the last mission you are stuck with those choices unless you make a new save file. That is so frustrating!  

The aspects of the game I did enjoy I can sum up with a few points:
- Parkour started out very clunky, but near the end of the game all of the options you have available to you really stand out
- The game runs very well and looks gorgeous
- The music I did enjoy quite a bit! The main "Run, Jump, Fight" theme throughout the game was a great addition!

Overall, I would only recommend buying the game if it were on a big sale and you are prepared to be disappointed in the end with the story.

DL2 overall in 2026 is still a pretty good game. While the games is without flaws, it was still an overall very enjoyable experiences. Parkour can be bit award sometimes, at least on MnK. Combat is impactful and gory, feel just as good as any hack n slash type of games.
Heck yeah of course I'll recommend this game, but purchase at a discount. 7/10

To be for real the first one has better zombies and better combat with bulk wepons but the second one has better parcour and human combat and the grappler is not op like in the first one but in all it is a great game 9,5/10 will play more of it ( I have like 200h on ps 5 )

Atmosphere and overall tone is way different from the first game. Volatiles feel nerfed but the combat and parkour is so much better than dl1. I feel like the first game introduced night much better and made it actually scary.

After all the fixes, this game is better than DL1 (w/ the exception of atmosphere, vibes, and being the foundation of the series) and I'm tired of pretending it's not. It pretty much fixes every issue DL1 had and expands on its systems.

People say the story is bad, and so did I until a recent replay, and while it's not amazing, saying that it's the worst ♥♥♥♥ ever is just disingenuous. It's fine. It's serviceable. The characters are likeable enough, and there are plenty of story missions that are bangers. By all accounts, DL1 (not including the Following) did a LOT worse, because not only was it done pretty poorly, it was also by the books to an asinine degree. So no, the story isn't THAT bad. I reject that. It's "Okay" w/ some cool moments at best, and "mid" at worst.

TLDR If you want a genuinely good game, DL2 is unironically just DL1 but better now. If you want superb vibes/setting, then yeah, DL1 still takes the cake, but I'd rather have a fun game first and vibes second.

EDIT: This goes ESPECIALLY hard if you're playing on Nightmare on a fresh save. In DL1, it's a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ slog (trust me, I did it) and it's clear you aren't intended to do Nightmare on a fresh save, despite them letting you. DL2 feels like Nightmare can be done from the rip and that it's a viable way to play.
10/10 game, gameplay is utterly amazing, storyline is extremely well-made and intriguing, and the art is one of a kind.

Personally, I love how they brought a lot of features in this game compared to Dying Light 1. The story in this game is not very interesting, not a fan. It also does not feel scary enough if we are comparing this to Dying Light 1. Adding more horror element in this game would make me like this game a lot more. The night-time is not as scary. I still recommend this game though, maybe try to get it on sale instead. Not worth the 60 dollars for sure.

8.5 / 10 . The graphics are on point . mechanics too , parkour's good and the combat is pretty smooth BUT the attention of detail is not the greatest . IMO Dying light 1 is better , although DL 2 is enjoyable too . Recommend it .

honestly, this game is a masterpiece. Take it slow, do all the quests, find the extra quests that are hidden and only activated if you listen to people and choose to talk to them and you will have a great time. My only problem is how often they use "oh no i passed out" as a plot device, especially towards the end... but... i'm still playing it again for a third time so, didn't effect me that much :P
satisfying gameplay, versatile skills, peak agility, deep story

Honestly Dying Light 2 doesn't really feel like the original Dying Light, but it's got its positives. The environment is fantastic, especially the lighting modes. I'm a sucker for the originals day and night cycle, and especially for the sunsets. While this one is def a downgrade on the cycles, it's still incredibly beautiful to look at. The parkour feels a little overly complicated, with all the keybinds being halfway across the keyboard or just random numbers, but it's pretty smooth. The music is fantastic, I like how the longer you have a parkour streak, the more intense it gets. I think my favorite part about the game is the soundtrack itself, it's genuinely fantastic.

However, I feel like the story is very lackluster and quite boring frankly. The constant busy work of side quests and errand-boying gets genuinely exhausting and makes the game extremely frustrating. The main plot sort of feels very generic, and there's literally so much fluff in the interactions that halfway through a conversation, I forget what we were talking about. They could have gone a different route with the story, maybe making Waltz more intimidating or an actual threat, because he feels very... I dunno, not very important? You kind of forget about him.
Not to mention there's so many side characters that somehow become important later on in the story. There's almost too much to wrap your head around when it comes to the actual plot and stuff.

Overall, it's a decent upgrade parkour and soundtrack wise, but not an amazing game. I'm on my fourth replay of the game, and while I used to despise it, it's grown on me, if only a bit. It's not as great story wise as the first game, with an unbearable amount of side-♥♥♥♥, but it's an OK addition to the series. The most fun thing about it is the parkour.

This is my review for Dying Light 2, I have to say this game is very bittersweet to anyone who has moved from DL1; but at the same time it introduces a new protagonist who is trying to survive this apocalypse. If you plan on buying this game, please read below.

Let's talk about what made this bittersweet, the game feels different from what DL1 was. You do not feel that distant howling from DL1 which can make anyone paranoid if they are out in the open. The leveling system is completely different and it mostly feels like Techland wanted us to play and grind this game in order to have any standing against the zombies. Unlike in DL1, I could take any path or whatever playstyle I prefer from the start. The gear system is very odd for anyone new, but it is quite understandable. I do not personally like the "Pilgrim outpost" system which is accessible for anyone but I am not a fan of it. 

Now let's talk about what makes this game good. This game introduces us to Aiden, we get to see his story and perspective of how someone who is not as gifted in military background as Kyle Crane survive in this world. Aiden represents survivors who are doing whatever they can to survive and his story definitely sticks out. I would say the story is not as good as DL1 but, it's definitely worth investing in! The execution of Aiden's story could have been better, some parts of it feels rushed or too far stretched; I would blame Techland for it but it's still worth it. 

So the ones who are planning to buy this game, I would recommend it. The game is still very fun, the humor and the gameplay is not on par with DL1 but it definitely feels better as you progress and play this game further. IT will feel very odd when you move from DL1 to this, but as you play further you will get used to it and master it. The DLC is also very awesome, it has some really great challenges for players who want to try something difficult. Dying Light 2 was criticized a lot, but it is worth giving it a shot. The microtransactions are annoying but you can enjoy the game without it. As for performance, my Laptop 3050 GPU was doing okay-ish during my gameplay.

That's all folks, I will see you all in DLTB. Do not forget, good night and good luck. Stay human!

Aside from steep comparisons to Dying Light 1 and The Beast. The game is large and offers plenty to do. If you take advantage of the Pilgrim Outpost you'll get a lot out of this game. Also pretty accessible if you're friends were maybe not too into the other 2 Dying Lights.

Loved the first Dying Light. Which is why I'm very confused how this has a similar level of positive reviews.

It's a completely different genre of game. This isn't an atmospheric thriller with gritty combat and parkour leaning into the combat.

This is like an MMO-like parkour game with a lame 'stay one second too long in the dark and you die' rule.

Everything that felt good about survival skills in DL 1 has been removed or nerfed to the ground. I suddenly need NPCs to make weapons for me at all. They ask INSANE amounts of money to repair ♥♥♥♥♥♥ weapons.

The combat feels off. The tutorial about vaulting over an enemy to stun him doesn't even work. We semi-accidentally aggroed human survivors outside the door of the first settlement, got into a fight and murdered 8 humans. 

The quest dialogue inside the settlement about the guy being accused of poisoning/murder right afterwards confused us greatly, as we assumed we were about to face judgment and would probably reload, but no. We laughed at this parody of a game and uninstalled.
Pretty good actually , solid 8.5/10 if you here for the gameplay but not story because it's so mediocre

Couldn't crane it in this game but that's okay the game is still good, parkour animations and the parkour is like the best ever seen in a game so far even beating mirrors edge and catalyst maybe cuz they put the guy who literally invented parkour in the game and asked him to help with the animations as well, the exploration is very fun and the story is alright but this game's best aspect is the parkour for sure and just running around the world so overall a 8/10 game,

The game is too dark, even on maximum gamma. I have tried multiple filters, but none of them have been bright enough for me to see. I have just come from Dying Light 1, where I could easily see what was going on during the daytime
I also cannot see what is going on during cutscenes, even with maximum gamma
Looking at these, I am not the only one who has had this problem:
https://steamcommunity.com/app/534380/discussions/0/3802776599342269136/
https://steamcommunity.com/app/534380/discussions/0/3810655600547452017/
The game is unplayable for me. I should not need to have the flashlight on during daylight in order to see something, let alone during cutscenes

Also, the parkour is floaty, and the combat feels less weighty than Dying Light 1

More updates this game has had, buggier it has become. Lost a save file due to zone load corruption where character just floating in the sky, either in fully black screen with some UI elements, or the graphics load in but all the same I cannot do anything to get myself unstuck.

60 hours of coop playtime lost and I'd like to emphasize that we've enjoyed the game, even though there's been alot of annoying little bugs here and there CONSTANTLY. 

This one was that broke the camels back.. thanks and bye.

The gameplay was a meh at best, I do like the additional parkour movement sets and animation but compared to the E3 trailer it was a massive downgrade, enough fun to play with your friends. The story though, the story in Dyinglight 1 was already mediocre enough they somehow made DL2's story worse. The ending was also rushed and left players with a blank emotions. 
The optimization is bad, you sometimes get teleport into the elevator with all of your friends outside so you cannot continue unless they teleport you, sometimes the bug won't even let you proceed with the story, the game crashes constantly every 5 minutes if you use framegen, every 2 hours if you don't.
Still recommend if you play with friends, if you play for the story however I highly not recommend

Una porqueria llena de bugs imposible jugar en modo cooperativo, el juego es mucho peor que el primer Dying Light, el combate es aburrido y malo, los zombies que antes eran poderoso e intimidantes ahora son de papel. La campaña es un desastre total, no guarda el progreso de tu partida ni las misiones completadas cuando jugas en modo cooperativo, el parkour es un asco total, los compañeros en modo coop desaparecen todo el tiempo, no los muestra a no ser que esten muy cerca, una cantidad interminable de bugs que hacen que te den ganas de desintalarlo cuanto antes, las armas son todas horribles y se rompen todo el tiempo, es aburrido de punta a punta, la calidad grafica es mala aun en ultra. No compren esta basura!!!
Not a bad game by itself but inferior to DL1. Environment and comabt is OK however the story, dialogue and repeatative quests let it down.
dont buy, ♥♥♥♥♥ fun, play dead island or give me 10 bucks for no reason
This game feels like a complete downgrade from the first. Can't imagine what happened during the concept.
